meaning: n.(旧时武将家的)家臣,扈从,保持者,[机]固定器
examples: The agency will pay you a monthly retainer.
Middle-class families contract with a puller to take a child to school and pick him up; the puller essentially becomes a family retainer.
Unless you buy an awful lot of art, keeping an adviser on retainer can get needlessly expensive.
If unbundling fees means firms having to pay a fat retainer for advisory work, so be it.
